reasoning:
The baseline for comparison is the value on 2025-08-31. Given the weekly Wednesday reporting, this corresponds to the closely preceding August 27 value, which saw a stark end-of-month drop to 3,216,851 (down from 3.328M on Aug 13). 

For September 7 (which captures September 3 data), there is historically a very strong start-of-month rebound in reserves as EOM settlements clear. Every month in 2025 has shown a jump in the first half of the month relative to the end of the previous month. The FRED linear model also predicts a bounce to ~3.3M. Thus, a value higher than 3.216M is extremely likely. (Prob: 0.75)

For September 30 (which captures late September data), the massive September 15 corporate tax deadline reliably funnels cash out of bank reserves and into the Treasury General Account (TGA). For context, in September 2024, reserves dropped to 3,141,565 by September 25. Given QT is still draining passively and the TGA is being refilled following the recent budget act, it is highly probable reserves will drop below the 3.216M mark. I moderate the probability to 0.30 to reflect increasing uncertainty.

For November 29 and February 2026, the baseline expectation is a continued gradual decline as the Fed aims for an "ample" regime (~2.9T target). However, long-horizon factors—such as the exact end date of QT (believed by Wall Street to be late 2025 or early 2026), shifting TGA management by the new administration, and potential repo market interventions—compound our uncertainty. Complying strictly with the instruction to increase uncertainty as the horizon lengthens, the probabilities for these distant dates are drawn progressively closer to 0.50.
